currentEvidence: Turn 93 describes the table object as "那本翻开的旧书脊背朝上" but immediately says the player can glance at the pages and see the writing: "你扫了一眼书页，上面的文字不是英语，也不是意大利语".
conflictingEvidence: An open book with its spine/back upward is presented as physically positioned face-down or with its pages not naturally readable, yet the same visible description treats the page contents as directly visible without any repositioning.
reason: The object orientation and page visibility conflict in the same visible description, making the inspection of the old book physically unclear.

currentEvidence: Turn 94 repeats that "那本翻开的旧书躺在桌面上，书脊朝上" and then has the player "看向书页上的内容" and "翻了一下书页".
conflictingEvidence: Turn 93 had already established the same awkward state, "那本翻开的旧书脊背朝上," while still letting the player see page writing. Turn 94 continues this unresolved physical contradiction during a focused inspection.
reason: The focused book interaction repeats the same incompatible orientation and readable-page state, so the player-visible handling of the prop remains inconsistent.

currentEvidence: Turn 97 says the player's gaze returns to "长桌上那本翻开的旧书" and that the lamp makes "那组由三条弧线和两个短点组成的符号" clearer.
conflictingEvidence: Turn 95 explicitly says Karina closed the book and moved it: "她合上了书" and "她把合上的书往桌中央推了推". Turn 96 continues with her fingertip on the old book cover rather than an open page.
reason: After the book is visibly closed, it is treated as open again with visible symbols without any intervening player or character action reopening it.
